WILLIAM (-before 25 Jul 1281, maybe before Jan 1279).  "…Willelmo filio Duncani comitis de Mar…" …

zh.Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William De Mar V Earl於1222年出生在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪時，他的父親Duncan MacMorgund 3rd Earl of Mar64歲，母親Orabella Latherisk37歲。他於1237年與Muriel Ferteth在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪結婚。他於1252年在英國蘇格蘭被列為lord chamberlain of scotland。他去世於1281年7月25日在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾吉爾德盧米享年59歲，埋葬在澳洲維斯特 奧斯特拉利亞未知 易曼德。

zh-hans.Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland: 苏格兰阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William De Mar V Earl于1222年出生在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪时，他的父亲Duncan MacMorgund 3rd Earl of Mar64岁，母亲Orabella Latherisk37岁。他于1237年与Muriel Ferteth在英国苏格兰阿爾迪结婚。他于1252年在英国苏格兰被列为lord chamberlain of scotland。他去世于1281年7月25日在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾吉爾德盧米享年59岁，埋葬在澳大利亚維斯特 奧斯特拉利亞未知 易曼德。

nameMeanings.name: Mar
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Gim, Wai, Fong, Hong, Man Chung, Moo, Mun, Quon, Wing, Yat, Bok, Chun.
Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Catalan: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mar ‘sea’ (from Latin mare).
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(w) ‘tender, delicate’, or an altered form of Mahr, a topographic name from mar ‘lake, swamp, stagnant water’.

variants.name: Martin
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William De Mar V Earl was born in 1222, in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, his father, Duncan MacMorgund 3rd Earl of Mar, was 64 and his mother, Orabella Latherisk, was 37. He married Muriel Ferteth in 1237, in Aberdeen, Scotland, United Kingdom. In 1252, his occupation is listed as lord chamberlain of scotland in Scotland, United Kingdom. He died on 25 July 1281, in Kildrummy, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, at the age of 59, and was buried in Unknown Island, Western Australia, Australia.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William De Mar V Earl est né en 1222, à Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, son père, Duncan MacMorgund 3rd Earl of Mar, avait 64 ans et sa mère, Orabella Latherisk, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Muriel Ferteth en 1237, à Aberdeen, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. En 1252, sa profession de son indiquée est lord chamberlain of scotland à Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 25 juillet 1281, à Kildrummy, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Unknown Island, Western Australia, Australie.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William De Mar V Earl 1222 in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Duncan MacMorgund 3rd Earl of Mar 64 und seine Mutter Orabella Latherisk 37. Er hat 1237 in Aberdeen, Schottland, United Kingdom Muriel Ferteth geheiratet. 1252 ist sein Beruf als lord chamberlain of scotland in Schottland, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb am 25. Juli 1281 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Kildrummy, Aberdeenshire, Scotland und wurde in Unknown Island, Western Australia, Australien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William De Mar V Earl è nato il nel 1222 nel Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Duncan MacMorgund 3rd Earl of Mar, aveva 64 anni e il suo madre, Orabella Latherisk, aveva 37 anni. Lui sposato Muriel Ferteth nel 1237, nel Aberdeen, Scozia, Regno Unito. Nel 1252, la sua occupazione è indicata come lord chamberlain of scotland nel Scozia, Regno Unito. Morì il 25 luglio 1281, nel Kildrummy, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolto a Unknown Island, Western Australia, Australia.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William De Mar V Earlが1222年にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアアバーディーンで生まれた時，父Duncan MacMorgund 3rd Earl of Marは64歳，母Orabella Latheriskは37歳であった。1237年にイギリススコットランドアバーディーンでMuriel Fertethと結婚した。1252年，イギリススコットランドでの職業はlord chamberlain of scotlandと記録されている。彼は1281年7月25日にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアキルドルンミーで59歳で死去し，オーストラリアウエステルン アウストラリアウンクノウン イスランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William De Mar V Earl(이)가 1222년 스컷랜드 압르디실르 압르디에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Duncan MacMorgund 3rd Earl of Mar(은)는 64세였고, 그의 어머니 Orabella Latherisk(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1237년 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디에서 Muriel Ferteth(와)과 결혼했다. 1252년, 그의 직업은 영국 스코틀랜드에서 lord chamberlain of scotland이었다. 그는 1281년 7월 25일 스컷랜드 압르디실르 킬드럼미에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 오스트레일리아 웨스터 오스트라리야 노운 이슬랜드에 묻혔다.

mn.Mar, Aberdeenshire, Scotland: Coontie o Aiberdeen, Шотланд, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William De Mar V Earl nasceu em 1222, em Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, seu pai, Duncan MacMorgund 3rd Earl of Mar, tinha 64 anos e sua mãe, Orabella Latherisk, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Muriel Ferteth em 1237, em Aberdeen, Escócia, United Kingdom. Em 1252, sua profissão aparece como lord chamberlain of scotland em Escócia, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 25 de julho de 1281, em Kildrummy, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, com 59 anos, e foi sepultado em Unknown Island, Western Australia, Austrália.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William De Mar V Earl родился в 1222 г. в местности абирдин, абирдиншир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Duncan MacMorgund 3rd Earl of Mar было 64 года, а матери, которую звали Orabella Latherisk, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Muriel Ferteth в 1237 г. в местности абирдин, Шотландия, Великобритания. В 1252 году он работал по профессии lord chamberlain of scotland в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. Он умер 25 июля 1281 г. в местности каилдрумми, абирдиншир, Шотландия в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности унноун исланд, вестирн аустраля, Австралия.

sm.from about 1252 to 1260: mai le e tusa o le 1252 e o'o I le 1260

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William De Mar V Earl nació en 1222, en Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, su papá, Duncan MacMorgund 3rd Earl of Mar, tenía 64 años y su mamá, Orabella Latherisk, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Muriel Ferteth en 1237, en Aberdeen, Escocia, Reino Unido. En 1252, su ocupación registrada era lord chamberlain of scotland en Escocia, Reino Unido. Murió el 25 de julio de 1281, en Kildrummy, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en Unknown Island, Western Australia, Australia.
